task_updates.executor_notes: Verified and completed the remaining outer-edge-only group-resize coverage around the existing resize refactor. `useClipResize` rejects pinned-group member clip resizes, `TimelineCanvas` derives `positionedShotGroups` from `group.start` plus `children`, and the updated canvas tests now cover hidden per-clip handles for group members plus overlay-handle resize behavior.

Preserved the approved shot-menu semantics and moved the final-video/image mode switches onto pinned-group child replacement. The updated switch tests now assert that `group.start` and row action positions remain stable across both image→video and video→image transitions.

Added `pinned-group-cohesion.test.ts` for random move/resize/replace sequences plus cross-track and clamp checks, and extended commit-boundary coverage so projected signatures are asserted explicitly. The existing `applyEdit({ type: 'config' })` projection test and sync-deferral re-arm test were rerun in the full touched-module pass and remain green.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed interior clip-member resizes are rejected in `useClipResize`, pinned-group members no longer expose per-clip resize handles in the canvas path, and `positionedShotGroups` reads `group.start` plus `children` instead of relying on clip-array order.

Confirmed Delete shot still calls `handleDeleteClips(...)` and then `unpinGroup(...)`, Unpin/Deconstruct still call only `unpinGroup(...)`, and the final-video/image switches now rebuild group children through pinned-group child replacement while preserving `group.start`.

Confirmed commit-boundary coverage asserts the signature from projected `resolvedConfig`, the pre-projection `applyEdit({ type: 'config' })` path still ends up projected, and the sync-deferral test proves the debounce re-arms until interaction state clears rather than skipping permanently.
